beginner.template: The {obj1} and the {obj2} are on the table.

upper_intermediate.template: The {obj1}, which I had placed {relation} the {obj2}, appears to have been moved.

advanced.template: The juxtaposition of the {obj1} and the {obj2} creates an unexpectedly harmonious visual effect.
One cannot help but notice that the {obj1} placed {relation} the {obj2} serves both a functional and an aesthetic purpose.

beginner.template: The {obj1}, the {obj2}, and the {obj3} are all on the table.

upper_intermediate.template: The arrangement of the {obj1}, the {obj2}, and the {obj3} suggests that someone organised them deliberately.
Had the {obj1} not been between the {obj2} and the {obj3}, the display would have looked quite different.

advanced.template: The spatial relationship between the {obj1}, the {obj2}, and the {obj3} reveals an underlying organisational principle.
One might argue that the positioning of the {obj1} relative to the {obj2} and the {obj3} is neither accidental nor arbitrary.
